Document:
# Two-Handed Melee
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Melee_Weapons"]
Two-handed melee weapons are slower to swing but deal significantly more damage. They require both hands to use, meaning you cannot hold a firearm at the same time — but in melee range, their power is unmatched.
## Spears
Spears are versatile two-handed weapons with reach. They can be thrown and deal good damage against armored and unarmored targets alike.
[bold]Wasteland Spear[/bold] — Simple sharpened pole. Cheap, effective, and throwable.
[bold]Tribal Spear[/bold] — Handcrafted tribal weapon with improved balance and a sharpened tip.
[bold]Flag Spear[/bold] — Spear adorned with faction colours. Ceremonial but fully functional.
## Hammers and Sledges
Blunt force weapons that crush through armor. The Super Sledge is the pinnacle of two-handed blunt weaponry.
[bold]Sledgehammer[/bold] — Heavy two-handed hammer. Slow but devastating against armored targets.
[bold]Super Sledge[/bold] — Powered sledgehammer with kinetic amplification. The ultimate blunt weapon.
[bold]Tribal Big Club[/bold] — Oversized wooden club. Crude but hits extremely hard.
[bold]Nail Board[/bold] — Plank studded with nails. Improvised but painful.
## Long Blades
Large swords and blades for those who prefer cutting over crushing. Many are tribal or ceremonial in origin.
[bold]Long Sword[/bold] — Two-handed blade with excellent reach and cutting power.
[bold]Tribal Sword[/bold] — Hand-forged large blade with tribal markings. Heavy and intimidating.
[bold]Tribal Machete[/bold] — Oversized machete built for two-handed swings. Common among Legion fighters.
[bold]Ripper[/bold] — Motorized chainsaw-blade weapon. Pre-war military close-combat tool.
